name: compone
documentation:
# Compone

Compone is a **Python component framework** inspired by React, that allows for
type-safe HTML generation. It is an alternative to Python template engines,
using Python objects instead of strings. This way you get Exception tracebacks
for mistyped HTML tags and attributes instead of malformed HTML!

functions.name: compone.html.helpers.classes
documentation:
Convert different types of HTML class arguments to a list of classes.
str --> list of classes
list of str --> list of classes
dict[str, bool] --> list of enabled classes

variables.name: compone.html.elements.inline_text.I
documentation: noqa: E741

modules.name: compone
documentation: ruff: noqa: F401

functions.name: compone.html.component._Elem
documentation: Create Component from HTML element on the fly.

functions.name: compone.html.component._SelfElem
documentation: Create Component from self-closing HTML element on the fly.

functions.name: compone.escape.escape
documentation:
Replace special characters to HTML/XML-safe sequences.
Marks the resulting string as safe with Markupsafe.

classes.name: compone.escape.safe
documentation: Exclude a string from autoescaping using Markupsafe.
This is more generic than HTML. Make sure that the API is future proof
in case we change implementation

functions.name: compone.robots.html_meta.MetaTag
documentation:
HTML meta tag for preventing robots to index a specific site.

noindex: prevents a page from being indexed
nofollow: prevents links from being crawled
noarchive: not to store an archived copy of the page
nosnippet: not include a snippet from the page along with the page's listing in search results

See:
  - https://www.robotstxt.org/meta.html
  - https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Meta_element#The_robots_attribute

classes.name: compone.htmx.constants.Event

class_variables.name: compone.htmx.constants.Event.ABORT
documentation: send this event to an element to abort a request

class_variables.name: compone.htmx.constants.Event.AFTER_ON_LOAD
documentation: triggered after an AJAX request has completed processing a successful response

class_variables.name: compone.htmx.constants.Event.AFTER_PROCESS_NODE
documentation: triggered after htmx has initialized a node

class_variables.name: compone.htmx.constants.Event.AFTER_REQUEST
documentation: triggered after an AJAX request has completed

class_variables.name: compone.htmx.constants.Event.AFTER_SETTLE
documentation: triggered after the DOM has settled

class_variables.name: compone.htmx.constants.Event.AFTER_SWAP
documentation: triggered after new content has been swapped in

class_variables.name: compone.htmx.constants.Event.BEFORE_CLEANUP_ELEMENT
documentation: triggered before htmx disables an element or removes it from the DOM

class_variables.name: compone.htmx.constants.Event.BEFORE_ON_LOAD
documentation: triggered before any response processing occurs

class_variables.name: compone.htmx.constants.Event.BEFORE_PROCESS_NODE
documentation: triggered before htmx initializes a node

class_variables.name: compone.htmx.constants.Event.BEFORE_REQUEST
documentation: triggered before an AJAX request is made

class_variables.name: compone.htmx.constants.Event.BEFORE_SWAP
documentation: triggered before a swap is done, allows you to configure the swap

class_variables.name: compone.htmx.constants.Event.BEFORE_SEND
documentation: triggered just before an ajax request is sent

class_variables.name: compone.htmx.constants.Event.CONFIG_REQUEST
documentation: triggered before the request, allows you to customize parameters, headers

class_variables.name: compone.htmx.constants.Event.CONFIRM
documentation:
triggered after a trigger occurs on an element,
allows you to cancel (or delay) issuing the AJAX request

class_variables.name: compone.htmx.constants.Event.HISTORY_CACHE_ERROR
documentation: triggered on an error during cache writing

class_variables.name: compone.htmx.constants.Event.HISTORY_CACHE_MISS
documentation: triggered on a cache miss in the history subsystem

class_variables.name: compone.htmx.constants.Event.HISTORY_CACHE_MISS_ERROR
documentation: triggered on a unsuccessful remote retrieval

class_variables.name: compone.htmx.constants.Event.HISTORY_CACHE_MISS_LOAD
documentation: triggered on a successful remote retrieval

class_variables.name: compone.htmx.constants.Event.HISTORY_RESTORE
documentation: triggered when htmx handles a history restoration action

class_variables.name: compone.htmx.constants.Event.BEFORE_HISTORY_SAVE
documentation: triggered before content is saved to the history cache

class_variables.name: compone.htmx.constants.Event.LOAD
documentation: triggered when new content is added to the DOM

class_variables.name: compone.htmx.constants.Event.NO_SSE_SOURCE_ERROR
documentation:
triggered when an element refers to a SSE event in its trigger,
but no parent SSE source has been defined

class_variables.name: compone.htmx.constants.Event.ON_LOAD_ERROR
documentation: triggered when an exception occurs during the onLoad handling in htmx

class_variables.name: compone.htmx.constants.Event.OOB_AFTER_SWAP
documentation: triggered after an out of band element as been swapped in

class_variables.name: compone.htmx.constants.Event.OOB_BEFORE_SWAP
documentation:
triggered before an out of band element swap is done,
allows you to configure the swap

class_variables.name: compone.htmx.constants.Event.OOB_ERROR_NO_TARGET
documentation:
triggered when an out of band element does not have a matching ID
in the current DOM

class_variables.name: compone.htmx.constants.Event.PROMPT
documentation: triggered after a prompt is shown

class_variables.name: compone.htmx.constants.Event.PUSHED_INTO_HISTORY
documentation: triggered after an url is pushed into history

class_variables.name: compone.htmx.constants.Event.RESPONSE_ERROR
documentation: triggered when an HTTP response error (non-200 or 300 response code) occurs

class_variables.name: compone.htmx.constants.Event.SEND_ERROR
documentation: triggered when a network error prevents an HTTP request from happening

class_variables.name: compone.htmx.constants.Event.SSE_ERROR
documentation: triggered when an error occurs with a SSE source

class_variables.name: compone.htmx.constants.Event.SSE_OPEN
documentation: triggered when a SSE source is opened

class_variables.name: compone.htmx.constants.Event.SWAP_ERROR
documentation: triggered when an error occurs during the swap phase

class_variables.name: compone.htmx.constants.Event.TARGET_ERROR
documentation: triggered when an invalid target is specified

class_variables.name: compone.htmx.constants.Event.TIMEOUT
documentation: triggered when a request timeout occurs

class_variables.name: compone.htmx.constants.Event.VALIDATION_VALIDATE
documentation: triggered before an element is validated

class_variables.name: compone.htmx.constants.Event.VALIDATION_FAILED
documentation: triggered when an element fails validation

class_variables.name: compone.htmx.constants.Event.VALIDATION_HALTED
documentation: triggered when a request is halted due to validation errors

class_variables.name: compone.htmx.constants.Event.XHR_ABORT
documentation: triggered when an ajax request aborts

class_variables.name: compone.htmx.constants.Event.XHR_LOAD_END
documentation: triggered when an ajax request ends

class_variables.name: compone.htmx.constants.Event.XHR_LOAD_START
documentation: triggered when an ajax request starts

class_variables.name: compone.htmx.constants.Event.XHR_PROGRESS
documentation: triggered periodically during an ajax request that supports progress events
Events can be used to modify and enhance HTMX behavior.

classes.name: compone.htmx.constants.Swap
documentation:
The hx-swap attribute allows you to specify how the response will be swapped
in relative to the target of an AJAX request. If you do not specify the option,
the default is htmx.config.defaultSwapStyle (innerHTML).
See: https://htmx.org/attributes/hx-swap/

modules.name: compone.robots
documentation: ruff: noqa: F401

modules.name: compone.robots.robots_txt
documentation:
Generating robots.txt and meta tags for search engine crawlers. See:
- https://www.robotstxt.org/
- https://developers.google.com/search/docs/crawling-indexing/robots/intro
- https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Meta_element#The_robots_attribute
